Bash(python ticket.py update ISS-036 --field status --value in-testing --caller Alessandro --note "[GATE 5] APPROVED. Repo structure correct and consistent with ISS-015 pattern. .gitattributes LF normalisation correct for Windows. Branch model \(main + feature/*\) appropriate - no develop needed, upgrade path documented. ai-standards.md accurate. Conventional Commits format observed. .claude/ exclusion correct. Workflow note only: issue_backlog.md versioned means frequent single-file commits - team aware. No blocking findings. Proceed to G6.")

Bash(python ticket.py update ISS-036 --field status --value po-acceptance --caller Dominick --note "[GATE 6] APPROVED. .gitignore covers all security-required exclusions \(coverage, pytest cache\). .env.example versioned correctly - no real .env present. .claude/ excluded correctly. nginx.conf and docker-compose.yml contain no hardcoded secrets - all env-var referenced. No credentials in versioned tree. Team reminder: never git add -f any .env file \(Constitution §8\). No blocking findings. Status -> po-acceptance.")

Bash(python ticket.py update ISS-036 --field status --value po-acceptance --caller Sofia --note "[GATE 7] PO ACCEPTED. All 6 AC met: git init in SitoPresepi2/ ✓, bare repo repos/SitoPresepi2-pm.git ✓, branching strategy documented in ai-standards.md ✓, initial commit on main ✓, ai-standards.md updated ✓, team session file written ✓. Awaiting owner final sign-off to resolve.")

Bash(python ticket.py create --type task --component process --title "handoff.py: structured append-only AI handoff writer" --urgency medium --project tooling --sprint tools-1 --owner Claire --tags "#tooling #handoff #ai-protocol" --notes "Build handoff.py CLI tool. Writes structured append-only entries to memory/handoff/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Required fields: date, ai, tickets_touched, gate_status, tests, blockers, next_action. Refuses to write if any required field missing. Appends to today file if exists, creates new file if not. Replaces the prose Handoff Note block in friendly_agreement.md. TDD required. Part of Opus analysis implementation \(ISS-037 plan F1-F5 + T1\).")

Bash(python ticket.py create --type improvement --component process --title "ticket.py create: add --session flag to G1 note for handover traceability" --urgency low --project tooling --sprint tools-1 --owner Claire --tags "#tooling #cli #handover" --notes "Add optional --session flag to ticket.py create. When provided, appends Session: <filename> to the auto-generated note. Closes the gap where gate notes record what was approved but not which session file holds the full context. Incoming AI reads ticket note and knows exactly which session file to open.")

Bash(python ticket.py create --type task --component process --title "agent files: mark .github/agents/*.agent.md as canonical role source for both AIs" --urgency low --project general --owner Alessandro --tags "#agents #roles #ai-protocol" --notes "Add header note to each .github/agents/*.agent.md file stating it is the canonical role definition for both Claude and GitHub Copilot. Update CLAUDE.md to point Claude to these files when a role is invoked, not rely on internal role table. Closes asymmetry identified in Opus analysis: Claude and Copilot could interpret the same role differently.")

Bash(python ticket.py update ISS-037 --field status --value in-testing --caller Alessandro --note "[GATE 5] APPROVED. 85/85 passing. handoff.py: prepend behaviour correct, _require_non_blank strips whitespace, --handoff-dir override makes CLI testable. Tests: 11/11, parametrised blank rejection covers all fields and blank types, subprocess end-to-end test present, tmp_path throughout. Non-blocking suggestion: add --- separator between same-day entries for readability. Proceed to G6 \(Chris\).")

Bash(python ticket.py create --type improvement --component process --title "handoff.py: add entry separator + 2 missing test cases" --urgency low --project tooling --owner Claire --tags "#tooling #handoff #tests" --notes "Three small items from ISS-037 G6 review \(Chris\): \(1\) add --- separator between entries in handle_write — prevents AI misreading across entry boundaries on multi-entry days; \(2\) add test for whitespace-only existing file \(handle_write branches on ''if previous:'' after strip — branch untested\); \(3\) add 3-entry ordering test to prove ''newest always first'' contract beyond 2-entry case. All low effort. Should ship before first real multi-entry day.")
